prompt: A long, narrow corridor stretches into the distance, its walls and ceiling lined with peeling, off-white panels that reveal patches of darker, weathered material beneath. The floor is a cracked, gray surface, littered with small debris and patches of green moss or weeds pushing through the fissures. Along both sides, rusted metal barriers stand at intervals, their surfaces corroded and bent. Above, a series of fluorescent light fixtures cast a cool, even glow, illuminating the scene. At the far end, a red sign with the word "EXIT" glows brightly, its light reflecting faintly off the surfaces. The perspective draws the eye down the length of the passage, creating a sense of depth and isolation. As the view progresses, the walls and ceiling begin to ripple and distort, their surfaces warping into undulating, wave-like patterns. The red "EXIT" sign appears to float and twist within this shifting environment, its edges becoming jagged and fragmented. The once-straight corridor now curves and bends unnaturally, the barriers and floor details becoming blurred and distorted by the waves of motion. The light fixtures above seem to stretch and warp, their rectangular shapes elongating and curving with the surrounding surfaces. The overall scene becomes increasingly abstract and disorienting, as if the very structure of the corridor is dissolving into a fluid, undulating form.
